data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Simplifies user interactions by minimizing choice complexity.
data.technical_spec: Design focuses on clear, concise options to enhance decision-making.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Conforms to the concept of chunking information to prevent cognitive overload.
data.technical_spec: Content is broken down into manageable pieces to enhance comprehension.

data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: Optimizes the size and placement of UI elements for easier accessibility.
data.technical_spec: Efficient UI designs reduce the time to interact with functional elements.

data.title: Tesler’s Law of Conservation of Complexity
data.description: Attempts to simplify the user experience without compromising functionality.
data.technical_spec: Essential complexities are managed behind the scenes via efficient processes.

data.title: Zeigarnik Effect
data.description: Encourages user engagement via interrupted tasks that prompt completion.
data.technical_spec: Subtly reminds users of partially completed actions or series.
